heroHeadline: Trump Threatens UK With New Tariffs Over Digital Services Tax as Trade War Expands to Key Ally
heroSubtitle: Trump escalated the global trade confrontation on April 24 by threatening new tariffs on the United Kingdom over its digital services tax targeting US tech firms — widening the trade war to a core ally. The move comes amid the ongoing US-Iran ceasefire (extended indefinitely on April 21) with the Arabian Sea blockade still in force, and Iran's IRGC having seized two container ships in the Strait of Hormuz on April 22. Iran nuclear talks remain in limbo with FM Araghchi refusing to negotiate 'under the shadow of threats.'
footerNote: Data sourced from White House, Federal Register, executive orders, court records, Al Jazeera, CNN, Reuters, AP, NPR, Bloomberg, CNBC, Axios, NBC News, CBS News, Fox News, Gulf News, The National, Newsweek, Nate Silver/Silver Bulletin, YouGov/Economist, Fox News, BLS, Peterson Institute for International Economics, Tax Foundation, CFR, EIA, Treasury, Goldman Sachs, Federal Reserve, OECD, Cato Institute, Penn Wharton Budget Model, IMF.
